title: Trace a CSS Defect from Reproduction to Regression Proof
summary: Trace a CSS Defect from Reproduction to Regression Proof retrieves prior skills in an original computed-style Grid overflow debugging case scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css debug method, grid track sizing, grid placement in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real computed-style Grid overflow debugging case artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the computed-style Grid overflow debugging case contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: computed-style Grid overflow debugging case.
steps.why: Trace a CSS Defect from Reproduction to Regression Proof makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in computed-style Grid overflow debugging case.

content.text: A repeatable CSS debugging method records the failing task and conditions, reduces the case, checks parsed and matched rules, inspects cascade and computed values, turns on box and Grid overlays, tests intrinsic sizes and overflow ancestors, changes one variable, and reruns retained scenarios. Random declarations can mask causes and create regressions even when the screenshot improves. Grid track sizing resolves fixed and percentage constraints, intrinsic contributions, min and max track functions, and remaining flexible space. One fr is a share of leftover space, not a viewport fraction. Auto minimums can preserve min-content width and cause overflow, so minmax(0, 1fr) is a deliberate opt-out when content may shrink and wrap. Fit-content caps growth without behaving like a fixed size. Grid items can use numbered or named lines, spans, negative indices, and auto-placement. These tools change visual placement but not DOM, reading, focus, speech, or copy order. Dense packing can visually move later items into earlier holes, so it is risky for sequential or interactive content. Start with meaningful source order and use placement only for presentation that remains understandable when CSS disappears. Apply it to computed-style Grid overflow debugging case.
